title: Tekton Events Relay Helm Chart Values
description: Configuration values for deploying tekton-events-relay, a webhook relay for Tekton pipeline events to SCM providers and notifiers

image.title: Container Image Configuration
image.description: Docker image settings for the tekton-events-relay container

tag.description: Container image tag (overrides chart appVersion if set). Should follow semantic versioning.

pullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for the container

imagePullSecrets.description: List of Kubernetes secrets for pulling images from private registries

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes secret containing registry credentials

replicaCount.description: Number of replicas for the deployment. For production workloads, use 2 or more for high availability.

nameOverride.description: Override the name of the chart. Affects resource naming but not the fullname template.

fullnameOverride.description: Override the fullname of the chart. When set, replaces the generated name for all resources.

commonLabels.description: Additional labels to add to all resources created by this chart

commonAnnotations.description: Additional annotations to add to all resources created by this chart

logging.title: Logging Configuration
logging.description: Logging configuration for the tekton-events-relay application

verbose.title: Verbose Logging Options
verbose.description: Verbose logging options (only available when level=debug, otherwise config validation fails)

server.title: HTTP Server Configuration
server.description: HTTP server configuration for the tekton-events-relay application

metrics_addr.description: Metrics server address (empty to use main server)

max_body_size.description: Maximum request body size in bytes

enabled.description: Enable rate limiting

burst.description: Maximum burst size for rate limiter

enabled.description: Enable webhook authentication

secret_ref.description: Reference to webhook secret for HMAC validation

secret_file.description: Path to the webhook secret file for HMAC/bearer validation

validate_timestamp.description: Replay protection: require an X-Webhook-Timestamp header (unix seconds) within timestamp_tolerance of the server clock

timestamp_tolerance.description: Accepted clock skew for replay protection (Go duration format)

tls.title: Native TLS Configuration
tls.description: Native TLS for the receiver endpoint; both files must be set, otherwise the server speaks plain HTTP

cert_file.description: Path to the PEM certificate (empty = plain HTTP)

key_file.description: Path to the PEM private key (empty = plain HTTP)

dashboard_url.description: Base URL for the Tekton dashboard (used in notification links)

dedupe_size.description: Size of the deduplication cache to prevent duplicate notifications

retry.title: Outbound HTTP Retry Configuration
retry.description: Retry policy for outbound HTTP calls to notifiers and SCM providers (exponential backoff with jitter, Retry-After aware)

max_attempts.description: Total attempts including the first request

store.title: State Backend Configuration
store.description: State backend shared by deduplication and the accumulator. The default memory backend is per-pod (single replica only); valkey and olric share state across replicas.

backend.description: State backend: memory (per-pod), valkey (external RESP server) or olric (embedded distributed cache)

ttl.description: Entry lifetime on remote backends (Go duration format)

address.description: Valkey server address (host:port); required when backend=valkey

bind_port.description: Port for olric data traffic between relay pods

memberlist_port.description: Port for memberlist gossip between relay pods (TCP+UDP)

peers.description: Peer list override; defaults to the chart's headless gossip service

dlq.title: Dead Letter Queue Configuration
dlq.description: Preserves events that failed with permanent errors for inspection (GET /api/v1/dlq) and replay (POST /api/v1/dlq/replay)

enabled.description: Enable the dead letter queue

path.description: JSONL file path (mounted as a writable emptyDir by the chart)

max_size_bytes.description: Maximum file size in bytes before oldest entries are dropped

volumeSizeLimit.description: Size limit of the emptyDir volume backing the DLQ

filter.title: Event Filtering Configuration
filter.description: Event filtering configuration to control which event types are processed

accumulator.title: Event Accumulation Configuration
accumulator.description: Event accumulation configuration to batch multiple events into a single notification

enabled.description: Enable accumulator

ttl.description: Time-to-live for accumulated events (Go duration format)

max_size.description: Maximum number of events to accumulate

scm.title: SCM Provider Configuration
scm.description: Source Code Management provider configurations for sending pipeline status notifications

notifiers.title: Notifiers Configuration
notifiers.description: Notification channel configurations for pipeline event delivery

slack.description: Slack notification channels configuration. Supports multiple channels with independent webhook secrets and templates.

name.description: Unique identifier for this Slack channel configuration

enabled.description: Enable or disable this Slack notifier instance

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the Slack Incoming Webhook URL (webhook_url key). Used for webhook mode.

bot_token.description: Slack Bot Token auth (uses chat.postMessage API). Mutually exclusive with secret_name.

token_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the xoxb- Bot Token

channel_id.description: Slack channel ID to post messages to (e.g. C1234567890)

username.description: Display name for the bot posting messages

when.description: CEL expression to filter which events trigger notifications

template.description: Go template for the Slack message body. Available variables: .State, .RunName, .CommitSHA, .TargetURL

teams.description: Microsoft Teams notification channels configuration. Supports multiple channels with independent webhook secrets and templates.

name.description: Unique identifier for this Teams channel configuration

enabled.description: Enable or disable this Teams notifier instance

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the Teams Incoming Webhook URL (webhook_url key)

when.description: CEL expression to filter which events trigger notifications

template.description: Go template for the Teams message body. Available variables: .State, .RunName, .Namespace, .CommitSHA, .TargetURL

discord.description: Discord notification channels configuration. Supports multiple channels with independent webhook secrets and templates.

name.description: Unique identifier for this Discord channel configuration

enabled.description: Enable or disable this Discord notifier instance

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the Discord Webhook URL (webhook_url key). Used for webhook mode.

bot_token.description: Discord Bot Token auth (uses channels/{id}/messages API). Mutually exclusive with secret_name.

token_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the Discord Bot Token

channel_id.description: Discord channel snowflake ID to post messages to

username.description: Display name for the bot posting messages

when.description: CEL expression to filter which events trigger notifications

template.description: Go template for the Discord message body. Available variables: .State, .RunName, .Namespace, .CommitSHA, .TargetURL

pagerduty.description: PagerDuty incident services configuration. Supports multiple services with independent integration keys.

name.description: Unique identifier for this PagerDuty service configuration

enabled.description: Enable or disable this PagerDuty notifier instance

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the PagerDuty integration key (integration_key key)

when.description: CEL expression to filter which events trigger incidents

datadog.description: Datadog event tracking configuration. Supports multiple instances with independent API keys and sites.

name.description: Unique identifier for this Datadog instance configuration

enabled.description: Enable or disable this Datadog notifier instance

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the Datadog API key (api_key key)

tags.description: Custom tags appended to auto-generated tags (state, context, namespace are added automatically)

when.description: CEL expression to filter which events are sent to Datadog

grafana.description: Grafana annotation notifiers: post deployment markers to the Grafana Annotations API

name.description: Unique identifier for this Grafana instance

enabled.description: Enable this notifier

secret_name.description: Kubernetes Secret containing the service account token (token key)

tags.description: Extra tags applied to every annotation

template.description: Go template for the annotation text

sentry.description: Sentry release notifiers: create releases and deploy markers for successful runs (version = CommitSHA)

name.description: Unique identifier for this Sentry instance

enabled.description: Enable this notifier

projects.description: Project slugs the release belongs to

secret_name.description: Kubernetes Secret containing the auth token (token key)

webhook.description: Generic webhook endpoints configuration. Supports multiple webhooks with custom headers and payloads.

name.description: Unique identifier for this webhook configuration

enabled.description: Enable or disable this webhook notifier instance

url_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the webhook URL (url key)

url_key.description: Key within the secret for the webhook URL

auth.description: Optional outbound authentication configuration for the webhook request

token_file_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the bearer/apikey token (token key)

token_key.description: Key within the secret for the token

username_file_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the basic auth username (username key)

password_file_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the basic auth password (password key)

secret_file_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the HMAC shared secret (hmac_secret key)

header.description: Custom header name for apikey auth (e.g., X-API-Key)

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers sent with the webhook request (e.g., authentication tokens, routing keys)

transform.description: Go template or expression to transform the event payload before sending

when.description: CEL expression to filter which events trigger webhook calls

tracing.title: OpenTelemetry Tracing Configuration
tracing.description: OpenTelemetry tracing configuration for the tekton-events-relay application

endpoint.description: OTLP endpoint for trace export (e.g., otel-collector:4318). Empty string disables tracing.

service.title: Service Configuration
service.description: Kubernetes service configuration for exposing the tekton-events-relay

port.description: Service port exposed to cluster clients

targetPort.description: Container port that the service forwards traffic to

annotations.description: Additional annotations to add to the service resource

serviceAccount.title: Service Account Configuration
serviceAccount.description: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) configuration for the application

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account

name.description: The name of the service account to use. If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template

podAnnotations.description: Additional annotations to add to pods

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root user

runAsUser.description: The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process

runAsGroup.description: The GID to run the entrypoint of the container process

fsGroup.description: A special supplemental group that applies to all containers in a pod

allowPrivilegeEscalation.description: Controls whether a process can gain more privileges than its parent process

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the container's root filesystem as read-only

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root user

runAsUser.description: The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process

runAsGroup.description: The GID to run the entrypoint of the container process

capabilities.description: Linux capabilities to add or drop

drop.description: Capabilities to drop from the container

add.description: Capabilities to add to the container

resources.title: Resource Management
resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests and limits for the container

limits.description: Maximum resources the container can use

startupProbe.title: Startup Probe Configuration
startupProbe.description: Startup probe to determine when the container is ready to accept traffic for the first time

initialDelaySeconds.description: Number of seconds after the container has started before probes are initiated

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to perform the probe

failureThreshold.description: Number of times the probe can fail before giving up

livenessProbe.title: Liveness Probe Configuration
livenessProbe.description: Liveness probe to determine when to restart the container

initialDelaySeconds.description: Number of seconds after the container has started before probes are initiated

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to perform the probe

readinessProbe.title: Readiness Probe Configuration
readinessProbe.description: Readiness probe to determine when the container is ready to accept traffic

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to perform the probe

affinity.title: Pod Affinity Configuration
affinity.description: Pod anti-affinity and node affinity configuration

context_per_task.description: commit_status only: TaskRun events post their status under <context>/<task>, yielding one independent check per task

labels.description: Declarative label effect for label actions: lists of labels to add and remove with optional colors. Supports both string format (backward compat) and object format {name, color}.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Hex color without # (6 chars). Empty = provider default.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)
Label with optional color (color ignored for removal)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Color field ignored on remove operations

remove.description: Labels to remove (absent labels are ignored)

owner.description: Owner or team responsible for this deployment

email.description: Contact email for the owner or team

podDisruptionBudget.title: Pod Disruption Budget
podDisruptionBudget.description: PDB for voluntary disruptions; rendered only when enabled and running more than one replica (or HPA)

enabled.description: Create a PodDisruptionBudget when running multiple replicas

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum pods that may be unavailable during disruptions (integer or percentage string). Ignored when minAvailable is set.

minAvailable.description: Minimum pods that must remain available (integer or percentage string). Takes precedence over maxUnavailable when set.

autoscaling.title: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
autoscaling.description: Configuration for horizontal pod autoscaling based on CPU and memory metrics

enabled.description: Enable horizontal pod autoscaling

targetCPUUtilizationPercentage.description: Target CPU utilization percentage for autoscaling

targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage.description: Target memory utilization percentage for autoscaling

enabled.description: Enable ServiceMonitor creation

additionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the ServiceMonitor

networkPolicy.title: Network Policy Configuration
networkPolicy.description: Network policy to control pod-to-pod communication

enabled.description: Enable network policy creation

valkeyPort.description: Valkey port allowed for egress when config.store.backend=valkey

metricsPort.description: Port allowed for Prometheus scraping when config.server.metrics_addr is set

metricsFrom.description: NetworkPolicyPeer list allowed to scrape metrics (empty = any source)

extraIngress.description: Additional raw ingress rules appended to the NetworkPolicy

extraEgress.description: Additional raw egress rules appended to the NetworkPolicy

enabled.description: Enable template ConfigMap mounting

unsafe.title: Unsafe overrides
unsafe.description: Escape hatches for chart safety checks

allowMemoryStoreWithMultipleReplicas.description: Allow replicaCount > 1 (or HPA) with the per-pod memory store. Causes duplicate/fragmented notifications; not recommended.

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Seconds allowed for graceful shutdown; keep above config.server.shutdown_timeout_sec

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClass for the relay pods (empty = cluster default)

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints for the relay pods (raw Kubernetes topologySpreadConstraints entries)

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables for the receiver container (raw EnvVar entries)

extraVolumes.description: Extra pod volumes (e.g. TLS certificates for config.server.tls)

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts for the receiver container

Action.description: An action to perform when pipeline events are received

name.description: Unique name for this action within the provider instance

type.description: Action type determining what notification is sent

enabled.description: Enable or disable this action

when.description: CEL expression determining when this action triggers

template.description: Go template for the notification body

mode.description: Comment posting mode: 'create' posts a new comment per event; 'upsert' embeds an invisible marker and edits the existing comment for the same run (idempotent). Supported by GitHub, Gitea and Bitbucket Cloud comment actions.

filter.description: Optional action-level filtering with allow/deny lists per resource type

labels.description: Declarative label effect for label actions: lists of labels to add and remove with optional colors. Supports both string format (backward compat) and object format {name, color}.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Hex color without # (6 chars). Empty = provider default.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Hex color without # (6 chars). Empty = provider default.

labels.description: Declarative label effect for label actions: lists of labels to add and remove with optional colors. Supports both string format (backward compat) and object format {name, color}.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Hex color without # (6 chars). Empty = provider default.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Hex color without # (6 chars). Empty = provider default.

labels.description: Declarative label effect for label actions: lists of labels to add and remove with optional colors. Supports both string format (backward compat) and object format {name, color}.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Hex color without # (6 chars). Empty = provider default.

oneOf.description: Label name (string format for backward compatibility)

name.description: Label name (supports Go templates)

color.description: Hex color without # (6 chars). Empty = provider default.

GitHubInstance.description: A GitHub SCM provider instance configuration

name.description: Unique identifier for this GitHub instance

enabled.description: Enable or disable this provider instance

base_url.description: API base URL for the GitHub instance

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (not recommended for production)

auth.description: Authentication configuration for the GitHub instance — use secret_name (token auth) or app_id/installation_id (GitHub App auth)

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the GitHub PAT (token key)

secret_key.description: Key within the secret for the token value

app_id.description: GitHub App ID (for GitHub App authentication)

installation_id.description: GitHub App installation ID (for GitHub App authentication)

private_key_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the GitHub App RSA private key

actions.description: Actions to perform when events are received

GitLabInstance.description: A GitLab SCM provider instance configuration

name.description: Unique identifier for this GitLab instance

variant.description: Deployment model: 'saas' for gitlab.com, 'self-managed' for self-hosted

enabled.description: Enable or disable this provider instance

base_url.description: API base URL for the GitLab instance

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (not recommended for production)

auth.description: Authentication configuration for the GitLab instance — use secret_name (Personal Access Token) or oauth2 (OAuth2 client_credentials)

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the GitLab PAT (token key)

secret_key.description: Key within the secret for the token value

client_id_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the OAuth2 client ID

client_id_key.description: Key within the secret for the client ID

client_secret_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the OAuth2 client secret

client_secret_key.description: Key within the secret for the client secret

actions.description: Actions to perform when events are received

BitbucketInstance.description: A Bitbucket SCM provider instance configuration

name.description: Unique identifier for this Bitbucket instance

enabled.description: Enable or disable this provider instance

base_url.description: API base URL for the Bitbucket instance (required for server variant)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (not recommended for production)

auth.description: Authentication configuration (variant=cloud: username/app_password or oauth2; variant=server: token)

username_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes secret containing the Bitbucket Cloud username

app_password_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes secret containing the Bitbucket Cloud app password

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes secret containing the Bitbucket Server personal access token

secret_key.description: Key within the secret for the token (default: token)

oauth2.description: OAuth2 client_credentials configuration for Bitbucket Cloud

client_id_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes secret containing the OAuth2 client ID

client_secret_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes secret containing the OAuth2 client secret

actions.description: Actions to perform when events are received

AzureInstance.description: An Azure DevOps SCM provider instance configuration

name.description: Unique identifier for this Azure DevOps instance

enabled.description: Enable or disable this provider instance

base_url.description: Azure DevOps organization base URL (e.g., https://dev.azure.com/myorg)

genre.description: Build status context/genre identifier for Azure DevOps

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (not recommended for production)

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the Azure DevOps Personal Access Token (token key)

secret_key.description: Key within the secret for the token value

actions.description: Actions to perform when events are received

labels.description: Declarative label effect: add/remove lists, entries support Go templates evaluated against the event

GiteaInstance.description: A Gitea SCM provider instance configuration

name.description: Unique identifier for this Gitea instance

enabled.description: Enable or disable this provider instance

base_url.description: API base URL for the Gitea instance

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (not recommended for production)

auth.description: Authentication configuration for the Gitea instance — use secret_name (Personal Access Token) or oauth2 (OAuth2 client_credentials)

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the Gitea PAT (token key)

secret_key.description: Key within the secret for the token value

client_id_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the OAuth2 client ID

client_id_key.description: Key within the secret for the client ID

client_secret_secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the OAuth2 client secret

client_secret_key.description: Key within the secret for the client secret

actions.description: Actions to perform when events are received

SourceHutInstance.description: A SourceHut SCM provider instance configuration

name.description: Unique identifier for this SourceHut instance

enabled.description: Enable or disable this provider instance

base_url.description: API base URL for the SourceHut instance

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (not recommended for production)

auth.description: Authentication configuration for the SourceHut instance

secret_name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing the SourceHut token (token key)
